title: The Secret Garden of Mrs. Rose
introKo:
이웃집 Rose 부인의 정원 일을 돕는 아주 건전한 이야기.
…표면적으로는요.

quiz.q: Rose 부인이 기르는, 마을에서 가장 큰 것은?

0000.name: 수줍은 새싹
0000.en: A week later, you wave at Mrs. Rose over the fence, but you stay on your side. Every Sunday she leaves a bag of vegetables at your door. Some gardens, you think, are best enjoyed from a safe distance.
0000.ko: 일주일 뒤, 당신은 울타리 너머로 Rose 부인에게 손만 흔들어요. 부인은 일요일마다 문 앞에 채소 한 봉지를 놓고 가요. 어떤 정원은 안전한 거리에서 즐기는 게 제일 좋다고, 당신은 생각해요.

0001.name: 그래도 다시 오는 손
0001.en: You were shy with the seed, careful with the water, and you asked for her hands when the zucchini felt heavy. But you did ask to come back — and now you do, every single Sunday. "Slow hands," Mrs. Rose tells her friends, "but he always returns."
0001.ko: 씨앗 앞에선 수줍고, 물은 조심조심, 애호박이 무거울 땐 부인의 손을 빌렸죠. 그래도 다시 오겠다는 말만은 했고, 이제 일요일마다 정말 와요. "손은 느려도 꼭 다시 온다니까요." Rose 부인이 친구들에게 말해요.

0010.name: 과묵한 큰손
0010.en: You said little all morning, but Mrs. Rose remembers one thing: you carried the biggest zucchini alone. "Still waters run deep," she tells the whole village. The heaviest baskets now wait just for you.
0010.ko: 아침 내내 말수는 적었지만, Rose 부인은 딱 하나를 기억해요. 제일 큰 애호박을 당신 혼자 들었다는 것. "조용한 물이 깊은 법이죠." 부인은 온 마을에 말하고 다녀요. 이제 제일 무거운 바구니는 늘 당신을 기다려요.

0011.name: 겸손한 수확꾼
0011.en: You began shy and careful, but by the end you carried the big one alone and asked to come again. Mrs. Rose loves that kind of growth. "Quiet in the morning, strong by lunch — my best vegetables grow the same way," she says.
0011.ko: 수줍고 조심스럽게 시작했지만, 끝엔 큰 걸 혼자 들고 또 오겠다고까지 했죠. Rose 부인은 그런 성장을 사랑해요. "아침엔 조용하다가 점심엔 힘이 넘치는 것 — 우리 집 제일 좋은 채소도 그렇게 자라요." 부인이 말해요.

0100.name: 정원의 장마
0100.en: It was your first time, and you were careful with everything — except the water. "No such thing as too much water, right?" you said, and Mrs. Rose still laughs about it. Her garden has never been so wet, and her rain barrel has never been so empty.
0100.ko: 처음이라던 당신은 모든 일에 조심스러웠어요. 물만 빼고요. "물은 많을수록 좋죠?" 그 첫마디에 부인은 아직도 웃어요. 정원이 이렇게 촉촉했던 적이 없고, 빗물통이 이렇게 빈 적도 없대요.

0101.name: 단비 같은 이웃
0101.en: Every weekend you come back, watering can in hand. You are gentle with the seeds but generous with the water — very generous. "When you visit," Mrs. Rose says, "my garden drinks better than anyone at a party."
0101.ko: 주말마다 당신은 물뿌리개를 들고 다시 와요. 씨앗에게는 다정하지만 물에는 아낌이 없죠. 아주 아낌없이요. "당신이 오는 날엔 우리 정원이 파티에 온 그 누구보다 잘 마셔요." Rose 부인이 말해요.

0110.name: 조용한 실력자
0110.en: You arrived shy, but the garden found your talents: deep water and heavy lifting, all done alone. You went home without asking for more, and that is the part Mrs. Rose cannot understand. "All that talent," she sighs at her gate, "and he only came once."
0110.ko: 수줍게 왔지만, 정원이 당신의 재능을 찾아냈죠. 시원한 물 주기와 혼자 해내는 묵직한 들어 올리기요. 그런데 더 오겠단 말도 없이 집에 갔고, Rose 부인은 그게 이해가 안 된대요. "그 재능을 가지고 딱 한 번만 오다니." 부인이 문가에서 한숨을 쉬어요.

0111.name: 늦게 피는 꽃
0111.en: You said it was your first time — and maybe it was. But by noon you watered like summer rain, carried the big one alone, and booked next weekend too. "Students like you," Mrs. Rose says, "are the reason I love teaching."
0111.ko: 처음이라고 했죠. 정말 그랬을지도 몰라요. 그런데 정오쯤엔 여름비처럼 물을 주고, 큰 것도 혼자 들고, 다음 주말까지 예약했어요. "당신 같은 학생 때문에 가르치는 보람이 있다니까요." Rose 부인이 말해요.

1000.name: 큰소리 새내기
1000.en: "I'm good with my hands," you said — then you asked about every little step and shared the heavy work. Mrs. Rose found it charming. "Big words, careful hands — honestly, the safest kind of helper," she says.
1000.ko: "손재주가 좀 있어요"라더니, 그 뒤로는 한 걸음마다 물어보고 무거운 건 나눠 들었죠. Rose 부인은 그게 오히려 귀엽대요. "말은 크고 손은 조심스럽고 — 사실 그런 일손이 제일 안전해요." 부인이 말해요.

1001.name: 약속을 지키는 남자
1001.en: You talked with confidence, worked with care, and kept your promise to return. Now every weekend has the same plan: her garden, your hands. "Some men only talk," Mrs. Rose tells her friends. "Mine shows up."
1001.ko: 말은 자신 있게, 일은 조심스럽게, 그리고 다시 오겠다는 약속은 지켰죠. 이제 주말마다 계획은 똑같아요. 부인의 정원, 당신의 손. "말만 하는 남자도 많은데, 우리 집 일손은 진짜로 와요." Rose 부인이 친구들에게 말해요.

1010.name: 울타리 옆 해결사
1010.en: Good with your hands and strong enough to carry the big one alone — that is how Mrs. Rose describes you now. You never asked to come back, but somehow her biggest vegetables always wait until you walk past her fence. Funny how that works.
1010.ko: 손재주 좋고, 큰 것도 혼자 드는 사람. 요즘 Rose 부인이 당신을 그렇게 소개해요. 다시 오겠다는 말은 안 했는데, 이상하게 제일 큰 채소들은 꼭 당신이 울타리 앞을 지날 때 나타나요. 참 신기한 일이죠.

1011.name: 떠오르는 에이스
1011.en: Confident hands, careful watering, the big one carried alone — and you booked next weekend before she could even ask. The neighbors keep asking who her new gardener is. Mrs. Rose just smiles and says nothing at all.
1011.ko: 자신 있는 손, 신중한 물 주기, 혼자 들어 올린 큰 것. 게다가 부인이 묻기도 전에 다음 주말을 예약했죠. 이웃들은 새 정원사가 누구냐고 자꾸 물어요. Rose 부인은 그저 웃으며 아무 말도 안 해요.

1100.name: 넘치는 물뿌리개
1100.en: You promised strong hands and poured water like a summer storm, but you shared the big one and left with a polite goodbye. Mrs. Rose still tells the story: "He came, he watered, he went home early." Then she looks at her very green garden and smiles.
1100.ko: 힘 좋은 손을 약속했고, 물은 여름 소나기처럼 부었죠. 그래도 큰 건 나눠 들고, 인사만 남기고 일찍 갔어요. Rose 부인은 아직도 그 얘길 해요. "왔고, 물을 줬고, 일찍 집에 갔죠." 그러고는 아주 푸르러진 정원을 보며 웃어요.

1101.name: 목마른 단골
1101.en: Confident from the first hello, generous with the water, and booked for every weekend after. The only thing you did not do alone was the zucchini — you asked for her hands, and she loved that most of all. "The best gardening," she says, "is done by four hands."
1101.ko: 첫인사부터 자신만만, 물은 아낌없이, 주말은 전부 예약. 혼자 하지 않은 건 애호박 하나뿐이었죠. 부인의 손을 빌렸고, 부인은 그게 제일 좋았대요. "정원 일은 손이 넷일 때 제일 좋은 법이에요." 부인이 말해요.

1110.name: 좋은 꿈 같은 사람
1110.en: Strong hands, deep water, the big one carried alone — you did it all, said her garden was amazing, and went home. Mrs. Rose stood at the gate for a long time. Some helpers, she thinks, are like a good dream: wonderful, and gone too soon.
1110.ko: 힘 좋은 손, 넉넉한 물, 혼자 든 큰 것까지. 전부 해내고는 정원이 멋지다는 말만 남기고 집에 갔죠. Rose 부인은 한참 문가에 서 있었어요. 어떤 일손은 좋은 꿈 같아요. 근사한데, 너무 빨리 사라지죠.

1111.name: 소문난 밤의 일손
1111.en: By summer, the whole village talks about Mrs. Rose and her garden — it has never looked so alive. She tells her friends you have the strongest hands in town. And the neighbors notice one small thing: her garden gate is never locked anymore — day or night.
1111.ko: 여름이 되자 온 마을이 Rose 부인과 그 정원 이야기를 해요. 정원이 이렇게 생기 넘친 적이 없었거든요. 부인은 친구들에게 당신이 마을에서 손힘이 제일 좋다고 자랑해요. 그리고 이웃들은 작은 변화 하나를 눈치채요. 부인네 정원 문이 이제 밤에도 잠겨 있지 않다는 걸요.

normal.text: Spring comes to the village. Your neighbor, Mrs. Rose, loves her garden very much.
People say she grows the biggest eggplants in town. Today, she calls you over the fence.
"My garden is hungry," she says. "I need strong hands. Can you help me this morning?"

options.en: Sure! I'm good with my hands.
Okay, but I've never done this before.
options.reaction: "Don't worry," she smiles. "I will teach you everything. Everyone has a first time."

normal.text: First, you dig a small hole. "Deeper," Mrs. Rose says. "Don't be shy. The seed needs a deep, warm bed."
"Now water it well. A dry garden is a sad garden. Everything works better when the soil is wet."

options.en: Like this? Am I doing it right?
options.reaction: "Perfect. Slow and gentle," she says. "You are a natural."

options.en: There is no such thing as too much water, right?
options.reaction: "Oh, honey," she laughs. "Exactly. In my garden, there is no such thing as too wet."

normal.text: Next, the zucchini. You hold it carefully. It is long and surprisingly heavy.
"What a big one!" Mrs. Rose says. "Size is not everything — but it never hurts."
"Now, into the basket," she says. "Can you manage it by yourself, or do you need my hands too?"

options.en: I can carry it alone. Watch me.
options.ko: 저 혼자 들 수 있어요. 보세요.
options.reaction: "My, my," she says. "Confidence looks good on you."

options.en: Can you hold it with me? It is heavier than it looks.
options.reaction: "Of course, dear. Four hands are better than two."

normal.text: You pick two round melons and put them in the basket. "Careful," she says. "Squeeze them softly to see if they are ripe."

options.en: They feel perfectly ripe. Your garden is amazing, Mrs. Rose.
options.ko: 완벽하게 잘 익었네요. 정원이 정말 대단해요.
options.reaction: "You have a good touch, dear. My husband never watered it enough — now look at it."

options.en: I want to do this again — can I come back next weekend?
options.ko: 또 하고 싶어요. 다음 주말에 다시 와도 돼요?
options.reaction: "You should! Gardening is like riding a bicycle — once you learn, your body never forgets."

normal.text: You go home with a basket of vegetables. Your body is tired, but your heart is full.
That night, you sleep very well. Gardening, you think, is the best exercise in the world.

hard.text: Spring settles over the village, and no one welcomes it more eagerly than your neighbor, Mrs. Rose, whose garden is her pride and joy.
People around town swear she grows the biggest eggplants for miles. Today she waves you over the fence with a meaningful smile.
"My garden has been terribly neglected," she sighs. "I could use a pair of strong hands. Would you spare me your morning?"

options.en: Sure! I have always been good with my hands.
options.reaction: "Wonderful," she smiles. "Somehow I knew you would be."

options.en: Okay, but I have never done this before.
options.reaction: "Don't you worry," she smiles. "I will walk you through everything. Everyone has a first time."

hard.text: First you dig a hole. "Deeper," Mrs. Rose insists. "Don't be shy about it. The seed deserves a deep, warm bed."
"Now water it generously. A dry garden is a miserable garden. Everything performs better once the soil is properly wet."

options.en: Like this? Am I doing it right?
options.reaction: "Perfect. Slow and gentle," she murmurs. "You are a natural at this."

options.en: There is no such thing as too much water, right?
options.reaction: "Oh, honey," she laughs. "Exactly. In my garden, there is no such thing as too wet."

hard.text: Next comes the zucchini. You lift it carefully; it is remarkably long and surprisingly heavy in your hands.
"My, what a big one!" Mrs. Rose exclaims. "Size is not everything, of course — though it certainly never hurts."
"Now, into the basket with it," she says. "Do you think you can manage it all by yourself, or would you like my hands as well?"

options.en: I can carry it alone. Just watch me.
options.ko: 저 혼자 들 수 있어요. 잘 보세요.
options.reaction: "My, my," she murmurs. "Confidence certainly looks good on you."

options.en: Could you hold it with me? It is heavier than it looks.
options.reaction: "Of course, dear. Four hands are always better than two."

hard.text: You gather two round melons and settle them in the basket. "Careful now," she warns. "Give them a gentle squeeze to see whether they are ripe."

options.en: They feel perfectly ripe. Your garden is truly amazing, Mrs. Rose.
options.ko: 완벽하게 잘 익었네요. 정원이 정말 대단해요.
options.reaction: "You have a good touch, dear. My husband never watered it nearly enough — now look at it."

options.en: I want to do this again. May I come back next weekend?
options.ko: 또 하고 싶어요. 다음 주말에 다시 와도 돼요?
options.reaction: "You should! Gardening is like riding a bicycle — once you learn, the body never forgets."

hard.text: You head home with a basket of vegetables, arms tired but heart full.
That night you sleep wonderfully well. Gardening, you decide, is the finest exercise in the world.

wave.en: to move your hand to say hello

sunday.en: the last day of the weekend

bag.en: a soft container for carrying things

enjoyed.en: past form of enjoy, to like

visit.en: to go see a person or place

teacher.en: a person who helps you learn

summer.en: the hot season of the year

friends.en: people you know and like well

notice.en: to see and become aware of

may.en: a polite word to ask permission

or.en: a word for choosing between things

ask.en: to say a question to someone

barrel.en: a big round container for water

booked.en: saved a time for later, from book

confidence.en: a strong feeling that you can do something well

dream.en: what you see in your mind while you sleep

gardener.en: a person who works in a garden

goodbye.en: a word you say when you leave

hello.en: a word you say when you meet someone

left.en: past form of leave, went away

lunch.en: the meal in the middle of the day

manage.en: to be able to do something hard

mine.en: the one that belongs to me

noon.en: twelve o'clock in the middle of the day

party.en: a fun event with food and friends

plan.en: an idea about what to do

promise.en: words you say you will keep

shared.en: did together, past form of share

shows.en: comes and is there, from show up

talent.en: something you are naturally good at

waters.ko: 물 — 격언 still waters run deep의 waters
waters.en: water, as in the old saying

watering.en: giving water to plants, from water

feel.en: to seem a certain way when you touch it

touch.en: the way you use your hands

introEn:
Helping Mrs. Rose with her garden. Perfectly wholesome.
...on the surface.

wink.manage: 크기 얘기가 나온 직후의 "감당할 수 있겠어요?"는 순수하게 들리기 어려운 질문이에요. 정원에서도, 정원 밖에서도요.
wink.four: 손 넷이 둘보다 낫다는 말 — 원예에서는 협동, 행간에서는 밀착이에요. 어느 쪽인지는 정원 주인만 알죠.
wink.heavier: 보기보다 무겁다는 말은 채소 칭찬이자, 영어 농담에서는 크기 자랑의 클래식한 다음 수예요.
wink.wet: '너무 젖는 건 없다'는 원예 격언집엔 없는 말이에요. 부인의 정원에서만 통하는 규칙이죠.
wink.bed: 씨앗에게 '깊고 따뜻한 침대'가 필요하다는 건 사실이에요. 침대라는 단어를 고른 것도 사실이고요.
wink.big: '크기가 전부는 아니지만 나쁠 건 없다' — 채소 얘기예요. 부인은 분명 채소 얘기라고 할 거예요.
wink.husband: 남편은 물을 충분히 안 줬다는 회고 — 원예 불만인지 다른 불만인지는 행간에 있어요.

winkEn.manage: "Can you manage it?" — asked right after a talk about size — is a question that struggles to stay innocent, in the garden or anywhere else.
winkEn.four: "Four hands are better than two": in gardening, teamwork; between the lines, closeness. Only the garden's owner knows which one she means.
winkEn.heavier: "Heavier than it looks" is a fine vegetable compliment — and in classic English innuendo, the natural next move after any size joke.
winkEn.wet: "No such thing as too wet" appears in no gardening manual ever written. It is a rule that works only in Mrs. Rose's garden.
winkEn.bed: It is true that a seed needs a deep, warm bed. It is also true that she chose the word bed.
winkEn.big: "Size is not everything — but it never hurts." She is talking about vegetables. She will absolutely insist she is talking about vegetables.
winkEn.husband: Her husband, she recalls, never watered the garden enough. Whether that is a gardening complaint or another kind entirely — that lives between the lines.
